heaviest_rainfall.status: The largest amount of rain ever recorded is on 09 November 2025 when 47.89mm fell at a maximum rate of 24.6mm per hour over the 144.8 hour event.

heaviest_rainrate.status: The heaviest rain was recorded on 02 September 2025 with a peak rate of 34.3mm per hour.  The storm lasted 15.0 hours and had a total rainfall of 21.19mm.

longest_rain.status: Rain fell for 144.8 hours on 09 November 2025 creating a new record that still lasts.  The storm produced a total of 47.89mm with a maximum intensity of 24.6mm per hour.

most_lightning.status: Since records began on 11 April 2025, the highest lightning strike count in one day is 23 on 07 September 2025.

storm.status: The last storm was on 17 April 2026 which lasted for 1 day.

top_10_dry.status: The ongoing dry stretch, 13 days and counting started on 18 April 2026 and ranks 3rd in length since records started in 23 September 2024.  The longest dry period started on 24 April 2025 and lasted for 19 days.

top_10_rainy.status: The longest rainy period started on 13 January 2026 and lasted for 22 days.

cold_snaps.status: There is currently no cold snap.  This year there have been 3 cold snaps, usually there are 3.7 cold snaps per year.

extreme.status: The record temperature difference stands at 27.7°C (-4.5 - 23.2°C), set on 24 April 2026.

heatwaves.status: There is currently no heatwaves.  This year there have been 0 heatwavess, usually there are 1.9 heatwavess per year.

tropical.status: The most recent tropical night was on 18 July 2025 and this has only occurred 62 times since 1973.

average.status: The current average daily wind direction is south-westerly (207°).  In the UK, a south-westerly wind often means mild and wet weather.

gusts.status: 128.2 mph is the largest wind gust recorded on 27 January 2026.

run.status: The top recorded wind run is 1737 miles on 21 October 2012.

speed.status: The record wind speed remains at 93.7 mph from 13 October 2012.

top_10_windy.status: The longest windy period started on 11 October 2012 and lasted for 21 days.

summary.description:
Today, a bit warmer for the feels like temperature (up 6.2°C) and outside temperature (up 2.8°C) and drier conditions in outside humidity (down 7%).

The past week and month had observable variation in outside humidity (up 21%) when compared to today.

Today’s average cloud levels is 898.5m higher than yesterday, 1051.3m higher than the past week, 2330m higher than the past month.

The cloud levels yesterday fell between 1406m minimum and 7120m maximum, with an average of 5100m; weekly and monthly averages were marginally higher at 4050m and 2770m.

summary.summary: Inside; temperatures should be quite comfortable. Outside; the temperature will feel nicely warm and pleasant. Also, dry and less humid conditions and very little to no wind will be present. Possible light showers, mostly clear skies with high cloud, and sunlight levels will peak at very high intensity. Similar weather conditions should remain.

washing.phrase: The conditions are favourable for drying your laundry outside right now but, as always, monitor the situation for changes to the weather.
